title: Tissue Punching Protocol
steps: Take brain out of -80° freezer and leave in -20 ° freezer overnight.

Make sure the cryostat is at -14° and the stage matches the cryostat temperature. If you plan on leaving the lid to the cryostat open during punching, you may want to turn the temperature a few degrees colder as the temperature warms up with the lid open.
Turn on cryostat light.
Get dry ice for tubes and crush it to fill a tube holder.
Label tubes for all desired regions.
Place blade in cryostat and make sure there is a good blade/plate ratio.
Find punching tools and place them nearby in crushed dry ice.
Remove blade.
Clear away discarded tissue sections.
Clean all chucks used.
Turn off cryostat light and set cryostat back to desired temperature.
Return the stage to the home position.
Put all tools away.
Clean surrounding area.
When you are finished with punching, place all tubes with tissue in the -80° freezer
Make tally marks as you complete punches for each region.

Place punching tool in tube that has a label that matches the punched region. Leave in tube for ~10-15 seconds and then punch the tissue out with punching rod. If you do not see the pellet drop, it could have melted to the side of the tool. Ensure the punch is frozen and collected at the bottom of the tube before moving on.
Cut the brain slice at 500um and take punches that correspond with your markings.
Use the punching tools to make a mark where you would like to punch. Please see attached images for regions collected.
When you start to see your region of interest, switch the cutting thickness to 500um.
Begin cutting the brain at 50-100um thickness until you get to region of interest. It is OK to adjust stage during this process to make sure you are getting a full slice of the brain.
Once the glue has been added and is completely frozen place chuck on the stage and adjust the brain to correct for any slanting that occurred while cutting the cerebellum.

Add layers of OCT compound glue until the glue is over the weak joint point where the cerebellum connects. When adding additional layers of glue, make sure that the previous layer has frozen completely before adding the next layer.
Cut off 1/3 of cerebellum to create a flat surface for the brain to stand on.
When you are ready to begin, move brain from -20° freezer to cryostat. Let sit for ~5 min.
ALWAYS PLACE THE PUNCHING TOOLS BACK IN DRY ICE BETWEEN PUNCHES
Make sure that the cryostat blade is sharp (must be sharpened at least every 6-12 months)
creator: McKenzie Pavlich
